name: validate-requirements
description: Validate that input meets prerequisites based on the user's saved standards for the project type. Use at the start of any quality pipeline to ensure the user has provided sufficient requirements.
capabilities: Check input completeness against project standards
Validate expected formats and required context
Return structured validation results with actionable feedback
author: AbeJitsu
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. Contains only markdown documentation defining how an AI should validate user input requirements. All 26 static findings are false positives: markdown code fences were misidentified as shell backticks, variable names with 'Rules' were flagged as crypto algorithms, and the GitHub source URL was flagged as hardcoded network address. Previous audit correctly classified this as safe with no dangerous patterns.
valueStatement: Poor requirements lead to poor outputs. This skill checks user input for completeness, clarity, and standards alignment before work begins. It acts as the first gate in a quality pipeline.
limitations: Does not modify or generate code itself
Cannot access external APIs or services
Relies on user having saved standards configuration
Provides validation only, does not perform the actual task

useCases.title: Validate feature requests
useCases.description: Ensure user stories and feature requests contain enough detail for development teams to act on.

useCases.title: Check code generation prompts
useCases.description: Verify prompts include necessary context, constraints, and examples before code generation begins.

useCases.title: Validate content briefs
useCases.description: Confirm content requests include topic, audience, format, and key points before writing starts.

promptTemplates.title: Basic validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use validate-requirements to check if my input has enough information to proceed. Report any missing details.

promptTemplates.title: Project-specific
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate my requirements against the project type standards. Tell me what I need to add or clarify.

promptTemplates.title: Code request
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if my code request includes: purpose, inputs, outputs, constraints, and examples. Suggest what is missing.

promptTemplates.title: Content brief
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my content brief for completeness: topic, audience, format, length, and key points to cover.

bestPractices: Use this skill at the start of any quality pipeline to catch issues early
Be specific about what is missing rather than just marking as invalid
Ask clarifying questions when input is close to valid
antiPatterns: Skipping validation when you think requirements are obvious
Accepting vague inputs like fix this or improve that
Rejecting input without explaining what specific information is needed

faq.question: What project types are supported?
faq.answer: Any project type with saved standards. The skill checks against standards.json for validation rules.

faq.question: What happens if no standards exist?
faq.answer: The skill performs general validation to determine if enough information exists to proceed.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with other skills?
faq.answer: Yes. Place it as the first step in a pipeline. It outputs structured results other skills can consume.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads user input and standards files. No data is sent externally.

faq.question: Why was my input marked invalid?
faq.answer: The validation result includes specific issues. Review the issues array for what is missing.

faq.question: How is this different from regular prompting?
faq.answer: It enforces consistent quality gates across all requests using your predefined project standards.
